import json
import re

# Read the reviews data
reviews_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Extract unique business references and map to business IDs
business_ref_to_id = {}
for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    # Convert businessref_X to businessid_X
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_ref_to_id[business_ref] = business_id

unique_business_ids = list(set(business_ref_to_id.values()))

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_reviews': len(reviews),
    'unique_businesses': len(unique_business_ids),
    'business_mapping': business_ref_to_id
}))

This is probably the worst moving company I have used in the Tampa Bay Area. I've moved 5 times since 2001 and I am in tears over this move. The movers unwrapped my furniture  down at the truck (was moving to a 3rd floor).  They were sweating profusely all over my items. I had to give them towels or continue to watch them saturate my couch, pillows, chair and ottoman. In addition they scrapped my brand new kitchen table against the cement walls in the staircase while carrying it upstairs.  I was told my items would be wrapped and protected. One of the movers had the nerve to tell me that this happens ALL the time.  I filed a claim with the company but was told that none of these items are covered under my contract.   I would be embarrassed to work for this company.  I work hard for my belongings and would have expected some respect from the crew. Horrible. Do not use!

We decided to go for the tasters dinner which was sold well by the staff. The food was excellent, bit the portions were so small. I should not feel hungry after a 66 dollar meal. Very poor value. Skip the tasters menu.  Will not come here again.

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all the data
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
businesses_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:32']

# Load businesses data
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Create mapping from business_id to business record
business_map = {}
for business in businesses:
    business_map[business['business_id']] = business

# Extract categories from reviews and count them
category_review_count = {}
review_count = 0

for review in reviews:
    review_count += 1
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    
    if business_id in business_map:
        business = business_map[business_id]
        description = business.get('description', '')
        
        # Extract categories from description
        # Look for patterns like "services in X, Y, and Z" or "including X, Y, and Z"
        if 'services in' in description:
            start_idx = description.find('services in') + len('services in')
        elif 'including' in description:
            start_idx = description.find('including') + len('including')
        else:
            start_idx = description.find('offers') + len('offers') if 'offers' in description else -1
        
        if start_idx > 0:
            # Extract the category part
            category_part = description[start_idx:]
            if '.' in category_part:
                category_part = category_part[:category_part.find('.')]
            
            # Clean up and split categories
            categories = category_part.replace(' and ', ', ').split(',')
            categories = [cat.strip().rstrip('.').rstrip(' and') for cat in categories if cat.strip()]
            
            # Count each category
            for category in categories:
                if category:
                    category_review_count[category] = category_review_count.get(category, 0) + 1

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_reviews_processed': review_count,
    'categories_found': len(category_review_count),
    'category_counts': category_review_count
}, indent=2))

args.code:
import json

# Get reviews data (list)
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']

# Get businesses file path
businesses_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:32']

# Load businesses data from file
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Create mapping from business_id to business record
business_map = {}
for business in businesses:
    business_map[business['business_id']] = business

# Extract categories from reviews and count them
category_review_count = {}
review_count = 0

for review in reviews:
    review_count += 1
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    
    if business_id in business_map:
        business = business_map[business_id]
        description = business.get('description', '')
        
        # Extract categories from description
        categories = []
        
        # Method 1: Look for "services in X, Y, and Z" pattern
        if 'services in' in description:
            start_idx = description.find('services in') + len('services in')
            category_part = description[start_idx:]
            if '.' in category_part:
                category_part = category_part[:category_part.find('.')]
            categories = category_part.replace(' and ', ', ').split(',')
        
        # Method 2: Look for "including X, Y, and Z" pattern
        elif 'including' in description:
            start_idx = description.find('including') + len('including')
            category_part = description[start_idx:]
            if '.' in category_part:
                category_part = category_part[:category_part.find('.')]
            categories = category_part.replace(' and ', ', ').split(',')
        
        # Method 3: Look for categories at end after last comma or in quotes
        if not categories and 'category' in description.lower():
            # Extract from single quotes or at end
            if "'" in description:
                parts = description.split("'")
                if len(parts) >= 2:
                    categories = [parts[-2]]  # Get text between last quotes
        
        # Clean and count categories
        for category in categories:
            cat = category.strip().rstrip('.').rstrip(' and').strip()
            if cat and len(cat) > 0:
                category_review_count[cat] = category_review_count.get(cat, 0) + 1

# Sort by count descending
sorted_categories = sorted(category_review_count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_reviews_processed': review_count,
    'categories_found': len(category_review_count),
    'top_categories': sorted_categories[:10]
}, indent=2))

args.code:
import json

# Get reviews data (list)
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']

# Get businesses data (list)
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:32']

# Create mapping from business_id to business record
business_map = {}
for business in businesses:
    business_map[business['business_id']] = business

# Extract categories from reviews and count them
category_review_count = {}
review_count = 0

for review in reviews:
    review_count += 1
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    
    if business_id in business_map:
        business = business_map[business_id]
        description = business.get('description', '')
        
        # Extract categories from description
        categories = []
        
        # Method 1: Look for "services in X, Y, and Z" pattern
        if 'services in' in description:
            start_idx = description.find('services in') + len('services in')
            category_part = description[start_idx:]
            if '.' in category_part:
                category_part = category_part[:category_part.find('.')]
            categories = category_part.replace(' and ', ', ').split(',')
        
        # Method 2: Look for "including X, Y, and Z" pattern
        elif 'including' in description and 'offers a range of services including' in description:
            start_idx = description.find('offers a range of services including') + len('offers a range of services including')
            category_part = description[start_idx:]
            if '.' in category_part:
                category_part = category_part[:category_part.find('.')]
            categories = category_part.replace(' and ', ', ').split(',')
        
        # Method 3: Look for "offers a wide range of services, including X, Y, and Z" pattern
        elif 'offers a wide range of services, including' in description:
            start_idx = description.find('offers a wide range of services, including') + len('offers a wide range of services, including')
            category_part = description[start_idx:]
            if '.' in category_part:
                category_part = category_part[:category_part.find('.')]
            categories = category_part.replace(' and ', ', ').split(',')
        
        # Clean and count categories
        for category in categories:
            cat = category.strip().rstrip('.').rstrip(' and').strip()
            if cat and len(cat) > 0:
                category_review_count[cat] = category_review_count.get(cat, 0) + 1

# Sort by count descending
sorted_categories = sorted(category_review_count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

# Get top 5
top_5 = sorted_categories[:5]

top_5_json = [{'category': cat, 'review_count': count} for cat, count in top_5]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(top_5_json))

import json
import re

# Get reviews data  
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']

# Check if we need to load businesses from file or use directly
businesses_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:48']
if isinstance(businesses_data, str):
    # It's a file path
    with open(businesses_data, 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    # It's already a list
    businesses = businesses_data

# Create mapping from business_id to business record
business_map = {}
for business in businesses:
    business_map[business['business_id']] = business

# Count reviews per business
business_review_counts = {}
for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] = business_review_counts.get(business_id, 0) + 1

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'reviews': len(reviews),
    'businesses_map': len(business_map),
    'businesses_reviewed': len(business_review_counts),
    'sample': dict(list(business_review_counts.items())[:3])
}))

This is probably the worst moving company I have used in the Tampa Bay Area. I've moved 5 times since 2001 and I am in tears over this move. The movers unwrapped my furniture  down at the truck (was moving to a 3rd floor).  They were sweating profusely all over my items. I had to give them towels or continue to watch them saturate my couch, pillows, chair and ottoman. In addition they scrapped my brand new kitchen table against the cement walls in the staircase while carrying it upstairs.  I was told my items would be wrapped and protected. One of the movers had the nerve to tell me that this happens ALL the time.  I filed a claim with the company but was told that none of these items are covered under my contract.   I would be embarrassed to work for this company.  I work hard for my belongings and would have expected some respect from the crew. Horrible. Do not use!

We decided to go for the tasters dinner which was sold well by the staff. The food was excellent, bit the portions were so small. I should not feel hungry after a 66 dollar meal. Very poor value. Skip the tasters menu.  Will not come here again.

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get reviews by 2016 users
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
businesses_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:56']

# Load all businesses
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    all_businesses = json.load(f)

# Create mapping: business_id -> categories list
business_categories = {}
for business in all_businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    description = business.get('description', '')
    
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern 1: "services in Cat1, Cat2, and Cat3."
    match = re.search(r'services in\s+([^\.]+)', description)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
    else:
        # Pattern 2: "including Cat1, Cat2, and Cat3."
        match = re.search(r'including\s+([^\.]+)', description)
        if match:
            text = match.group(1)
        else:
            # Pattern 3: try to find categories at the end
            text = description
    
    # Clean up the text
    if 'Located at' in text:
        # Find the last part after location info
        parts = text.split('Located at')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            text = parts[1]
            # Find category indicators
            if 'services in' in text:
                text = text.split('services in')[1]
            elif 'including' in text:
                text = text.split('including')[1]
    
    # Split by comma, 'and', '&', or period
    parts = re.split(r',|\band\b|&|\.|\|', text)
    
    for part in parts:
        cat = part.strip().rstrip('.').strip()
        # Remove common prefixes
        cat = re.sub(r'^(offers|providing|featuring|a range of|a wide range of|a delightful selection of|a diverse selection of|a diverse menu featuring|specializes in|offers enthusiasts)\s*', '', cat, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
        cat = cat.strip()
        
        if cat and len(cat) > 2 and cat not in ['Located at', 'this facility', 'this establishment', 'this business', 'this vibrant spot', 'this delightful spot', 'this charming establishment', 'this lively establishment']:
            # Remove location-based words at the end
            cat = re.sub(r'\bin\s+[A-Z][a-z]+\s*,\s*[A-Z]{2}$', '', cat)
            cat = cat.strip()
            if cat:
                categories.append(cat)
    
    if categories:
        business_categories[business_id] = categories

# Now count reviews per category
category_counts = {}
business_review_counts = {}

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    
    # Count reviews per business
    business_review_counts[business_id] = business_review_counts.get(business_id, 0) + 1
    
    # Add to category counts
    if business_id in business_categories:
        for cat in business_categories[business_id]:
            category_counts[cat] = category_counts.get(cat, 0) + 1

# Get top 5 categories
top_5 = sorted(category_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:5]

result = {
    'total_reviews_by_2016_users': len(reviews),
    'unique_businesses_reviewed': len(business_review_counts),
    'categories_with_reviews': len(category_counts),
    'top_5_categories': [{'category': cat, 'review_count': count} for cat, count in top_5]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Get reviews by 2016 users
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
businesses_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:56']

# Load all businesses
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    all_businesses = json.load(f)

# Create mapping: business_id -> categories list
business_categories = {}
for business in all_businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    description = business.get('description', '')
    
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern 1: "services in Cat1, Cat2, and Cat3."
    match = re.search(r'services in\s+([^\.]+)', description)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
    else:
        # Pattern 2: "including Cat1, Cat2, and Cat3."
        match = re.search(r'including\s+([^\.]+)', description)
        if match:
            text = match.group(1)
        else:
            # Pattern 3: try to find categories at the end
            text = description
    
    # Clean up the text
    if 'Located at' in text:
        # Find the last part after location info
        parts = text.split('Located at')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            text = parts[1]
            # Find category indicators
            if 'services in' in text:
                text = text.split('services in')[1]
            elif 'including' in text:
                text = text.split('including')[1]
    
    # Split by comma, 'and', '&', or period
    parts = re.split(r',|\band\b|&|\.|\|', text)
    
    for part in parts:
        cat = part.strip().rstrip('.').strip()
        # Remove common prefixes
        cat = re.sub(r'^(offers|providing|featuring|a range of|a wide range of|a delightful selection of|a diverse selection of|a diverse menu featuring|specializes in|offers enthusiasts)\s*', '', cat, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
        cat = cat.strip()
        
        if cat and len(cat) > 2 and cat not in ['Located at', 'this facility', 'this establishment', 'this business', 'this vibrant spot', 'this delightful spot', 'this charming establishment', 'this lively establishment']:
            # Remove location-based words at the end
            cat = re.sub(r'\bin\s+[A-Z][a-z]+\s*,\s*[A-Z]{2}$', '', cat)
            cat = cat.strip()
            if cat:
                categories.append(cat)
    
    if categories:
        business_categories[business_id] = categories

# Now count reviews per category
category_counts = {}
business_review_counts = {}

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    
    # Count reviews per business
    business_review_counts[business_id] = business_review_counts.get(business_id, 0) + 1
    
    # Add to category counts
    if business_id in business_categories:
        for cat in business_categories[business_id]:
            category_counts[cat] = category_counts.get(cat, 0) + 1

# Get top 5 categories
top_5 = sorted(category_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:5]

result = {
    'total_reviews_by_2016_users': len(reviews),
    'unique_businesses_reviewed': len(business_review_counts),
    'categories_with_reviews': len(category_counts),
    'top_5_categories': [{'category': cat, 'review_count': count} for cat, count in top_5]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

sample_reviews.text: Disappointed!  The gummy candies were stale and over priced!!   Fun to look at all the different candy but  won't be back!

This is probably the worst moving company I have used in the Tampa Bay Area. I've moved 5 times since 2001 and I am in tears over this move. The movers unwrapped my furniture  down at the truck (was moving to a 3rd floor).  They were sweating profusely all over my items. I had to give them towels or continue to watch them saturate my couch, pillows, chair and ottoman. In addition they scrapped my brand new kitchen table against the cement walls in the staircase while carrying it upstairs.  I was told my items would be wrapped and protected. One of the movers had the nerve to tell me that this happens ALL the time.  I filed a claim with the company but was told that none of these items are covered under my contract.   I would be embarrassed to work for this company.  I work hard for my belongings and would have expected some respect from the crew. Horrible. Do not use!

We decided to go for the tasters dinner which was sold well by the staff. The food was excellent, bit the portions were so small. I should not feel hungry after a 66 dollar meal. Very poor value. Skip the tasters menu.  Will not come here again.
